mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate over the prior month lowers the equity discount rate and supports multiple expansion, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when policy is easing.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of months with a 1-month FEDFUNDS decline, or rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the signal.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, the market is in a restrictive regime and higher real-rate pressure compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce negative 5-day returns at least 55% of the time, or if the conditional mean return is not below zero across any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening income and demand expectations, so when unemployment increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered events fail to show negative 20-day returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the average conditional return is nonnegative for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized upswing in RSAFS and HOUST signals stronger final demand and housing-led spillovers, which lifts cyclical earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-high-demand condition does not outperform the unconditional market in 60-day returns, or if either series alone explains the effect and the interaction term adds no incremental predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: Falling INDPRO reflects an industrial slowdown that weakens cyclical revenue momentum and feeds lower earnings revisions, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 20 trading days when industrial production deteriorates.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not negative in at least 55% of INDPRO-decline episodes, or if the signal loses significance after one-year rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is weak but CPIAUCSL remains elevated, the mix implies stagflation pressure: nominal rates stay restrictive while real growth softens, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth below 1% annualized and CPIAUCSL inflation above 3%
failure_condition: Reject if the low-growth/high-inflation regime does not produce materially lower 60-day returns than either condition alone, or if conditional downside frequency stays below 60% over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A stable or falling PAYEMS trend supports household income and consumption expectations, which sustains risk appetite and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS momentum does not correspond to positive 5-day returns more often than not, or if the conditional mean return is not above the unconditional mean by at least 25 bps.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising RSAFS and falling UNRATE marks a broadening demand impulse that supports earnings breadth and lifts the S&P 500 over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the paired demand-labor improvement fails to outperform single-variable signals, or if triggered months do not show positive 20-day returns in at least 60% of observations.
